heading: Required reporting of certain cyber incidents
label: 681b Required reporting of certain cyber incidents
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (c)(2)(C)(ii), was repealed by . See . References to terms defined in this chapter deemed to be references to those terms as defined in , see , set out as a Rule of Construction note under .Section 681(14)(A) of this titlesection 7143(b)(2)(N)(v) of Pub. No. 14028, , 86 F.R. 26633, which is set out as a note under , Public Printing and Documents.May 12, 2021section 3551 of Title 44

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A covered entity that experiences a covered cyber incident shall report the covered cyber incident to the Agency not later than 72 hours after the covered entity reasonably believes that the covered cyber incident has occurred.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The Director may not require reporting under subparagraph (A) any earlier than 72 hours after the covered entity reasonably believes that a covered cyber incident has occurred.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A covered entity that makes a ransom payment as the result of a ransomware attack against the covered entity shall report the payment to the Agency not later than 24 hours after the ransom payment has been made.

children.heading: Application
children.label: B Application
children.text: The requirements under subparagraph (A) shall apply even if the ransomware attack is not a covered cyber incident subject to the reporting requirements under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Supplemental reports
children.label: 3 Supplemental reports
children.text: A covered entity shall promptly submit to the Agency an update or supplement to a previously submitted covered cyber incident report if substantial new or different information becomes available or if the covered entity makes a ransom payment after submitting a covered cyber incident report required under paragraph (1), until such date that such covered entity notifies the Agency that the covered cyber incident at issue has concluded and has been fully mitigated and resolved.

children.heading: Preservation of information
children.label: 4 Preservation of information
children.text: Any covered entity subject to requirements of paragraph (1), (2), or (3) shall preserve data relevant to the covered cyber incident or ransom payment in accordance with procedures established in the final rule issued pursuant to subsection (b).

children.heading: Reporting of covered cyber incident with ransom payment
children.label: A Reporting of covered cyber incident with ransom payment
children.text: If a covered entity is the victim of a covered cyber incident and makes a ransom payment prior to the 72 hour requirement under paragraph (1), such that the reporting requirements under paragraphs (1) and (2) both apply, the covered entity may submit a single report to satisfy the requirements of both paragraphs in accordance with procedures established in the final rule issued pursuant to subsection (b).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to the limitation described in clause (ii), where the Agency has an agreement in place that satisfies the requirements of , the requirements under paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) shall not apply to a covered entity required by law, regulation, or contract to report substantially similar information to another Federal agency within a substantially similar timeframe.section 681g(a) of this title

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: The exemption in clause (i) shall take effect with respect to a covered entity once an agency agreement and sharing mechanism is in place between the Agency and the respective Federal agency, pursuant to .section 681g(a) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: exempt a covered entity from the reporting requirements under paragraph (3) unless the supplemental report also meets the requirements of clauses (i) and (ii) of this paragraph;1So in original. Probably should be “subparagraph”.1

children.label: II
children.text: prevent the Agency from contacting an entity submitting information to another Federal agency that is provided to the Agency pursuant to ; orsection 681g of this title

children.label: III
children.text: prevent an entity from communicating with the Agency.

children.heading: Domain name system
children.label: C Domain name system
children.text: The requirements under paragraphs (1), (2) and (3) shall not apply to a covered entity or the functions of a covered entity that the Director determines constitute critical infrastructure owned, operated, or governed by multi-stakeholder organizations that develop, implement, and enforce policies concerning the Domain Name System, such as the Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers or the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority.

children.heading: Manner, timing, and form of reports
children.label: 6 Manner, timing, and form of reports
children.text: Reports made under paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) shall be made in the manner and form, and within the time period in the case of reports made under paragraph (3), prescribed in the final rule issued pursuant to subsection (b).

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: 7 Effective date
children.text: Paragraphs (1) through (4) shall take effect on the dates prescribed in the final rule issued pursuant to subsection (b).

children.heading: Notice of proposed rulemaking
children.label: 1 Notice of proposed rulemaking
children.text: Not later than 24 months after , the Director, in consultation with Sector Risk Management Agencies, the Department of Justice, and other Federal agencies, shall publish in the Federal Register a notice of proposed rulemaking to implement subsection (a).March 15, 2022

children.heading: Final rule
children.label: 2 Final rule
children.text: Not later than 18 months after publication of the notice of proposed rulemaking under paragraph (1), the Director shall issue a final rule to implement subsection (a).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Director is authorized to issue regulations to amend or revise the final rule issued pursuant to paragraph (2).

children.heading: Procedures
children.label: B Procedures
children.text: Any subsequent rules issued under subparagraph (A) shall comply with the requirements under chapter 5 of title 5, including the issuance of a notice of proposed rulemaking under section 553 of such title.

children.label: A
children.text: the consequences that disruption to or compromise of such an entity could cause to national security, economic security, or public health and safety;

children.label: B
children.text: the likelihood that such an entity may be targeted by a malicious cyber actor, including a foreign country; and

children.label: C
children.text: the extent to which damage, disruption, or unauthorized access to such an entity, including the accessing of sensitive cybersecurity vulnerability information or penetration testing tools or techniques, will likely enable the disruption of the reliable operation of critical infrastructure.

children.label: i
children.text: a cyber incident that leads to substantial loss of confidentiality, integrity, or availability of such information system or network, or a serious impact on the safety and resiliency of operational systems and processes;

children.label: I
children.text: an information system or network; or

children.label: II
children.text: an operational technology system or process; or

children.label: iii
children.text: unauthorized access or disruption of business or industrial operations due to loss of service facilitated through, or caused by, a compromise of a cloud service provider, managed service provider, or other third-party data hosting provider or by a supply chain compromise;

children.label: i
children.text: the sophistication or novelty of the tactics used to perpetrate such a cyber incident, as well as the type, volume, and sensitivity of the data at issue;

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of individuals directly or indirectly affected or potentially affected by such a cyber incident; and

children.label: iii
children.text: potential impacts on industrial control systems, such as supervisory control and data acquisition systems, distributed control systems, and programmable logic controllers; and

children.label: i
children.text: any event where the cyber incident is perpetrated in good faith by an entity in response to a specific request by the owner or operator of the information system; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the threat of disruption as extortion, as described in section 681(14)(A) of this title.3See References in Text note below.3

A requirement that, if a covered cyber incident or a ransom payment occurs following an exempted threat described in paragraph (2)(C)(ii), the covered entity shall comply with the requirements in this part in reporting the covered cyber incident or ransom payment.

children.label: i
children.text: identification and a description of the function of the affected information systems, networks, or devices that were, or are reasonably believed to have been, affected by such cyber incident;

children.label: ii
children.text: a description of the unauthorized access with substantial loss of confidentiality, integrity, or availability of the affected information system or network or disruption of business or industrial operations;

children.label: iii
children.text: the estimated date range of such incident; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the impact to the operations of the covered entity.

children.label: B
children.text: Where applicable, a description of the vulnerabilities exploited and the security defenses that were in place, as well as the tactics, techniques, and procedures used to perpetrate the covered cyber incident.

children.label: C
children.text: Where applicable, any identifying or contact information related to each actor reasonably believed to be responsible for such cyber incident.

children.label: D
children.text: Where applicable, identification of the category or categories of information that were, or are reasonably believed to have been, accessed or acquired by an unauthorized person.

children.label: E
children.text: The name and other information that clearly identifies the covered entity impacted by the covered cyber incident, including, as applicable, the State of incorporation or formation of the covered entity, trade names, legal names, or other identifiers.

children.label: F
children.text: Contact information, such as telephone number or electronic mail address, that the Agency may use to contact the covered entity or an authorized agent of such covered entity, or, where applicable, the service provider of such covered entity acting with the express permission of, and at the direction of, the covered entity to assist with compliance with the requirements of this part.

children.label: A
children.text: A description of the ransomware attack, including the estimated date range of the attack.

children.label: B
children.text: Where applicable, a description of the vulnerabilities, tactics, techniques, and procedures used to perpetrate the ransomware attack.

children.label: C
children.text: Where applicable, any identifying or contact information related to the actor or actors reasonably believed to be responsible for the ransomware attack.

children.label: D
children.text: The name and other information that clearly identifies the covered entity that made the ransom payment or on whose behalf the payment was made.

children.label: E
children.text: Contact information, such as telephone number or electronic mail address, that the Agency may use to contact the covered entity that made the ransom payment or an authorized agent of such covered entity, or, where applicable, the service provider of such covered entity acting with the express permission of, and at the direction of, that covered entity to assist with compliance with the requirements of this part.

children.label: F
children.text: The date of the ransom payment.

children.label: G
children.text: The ransom payment demand, including the type of virtual currency or other commodity requested, if applicable.

children.label: H
children.text: The ransom payment instructions, including information regarding where to send the payment, such as the virtual currency address or physical address the funds were requested to be sent to, if applicable.

children.label: I
children.text: The amount of the ransom payment.

A clear description of the types of data required to be preserved pursuant to subsection (a)(4), the period of time for which the data is required to be preserved, and allowable uses, processes, and procedures.

children.label: B
children.text: consider any existing regulatory reporting requirements similar in scope, purpose, and timing to the reporting requirements to which such a covered entity may also be subject, and make efforts to harmonize the timing and contents of any such reports to the maximum extent practicable;

children.label: C
children.text: balance the need for situational awareness with the ability of the covered entity to conduct cyber incident response and investigations; and

children.label: D
children.text: provide a clear description of what constitutes substantial new or different information.

children.label: A
children.text: entities, including third parties pursuant to subsection (d)(1), to submit reports required by paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) of subsection (a), including the manner and form thereof, which shall include, at a minimum, a concise, user-friendly web-based form;

children.label: i
children.text: the enforcement provisions of , including with respect to the issuance, service, withdrawal, referral process, and enforcement of subpoenas, appeals and due process procedures;section 681d of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: other available enforcement mechanisms including acquisition, suspension and debarment procedures; and

children.label: iii
children.text: other aspects of noncompliance;

children.label: C
children.text: implementing the exceptions provided in subsection (a)(5); and

children.label: D
children.text: protecting privacy and civil liberties consistent with processes adopted pursuant to and anonymizing and safeguarding, or no longer retaining, information received and disclosed through covered cyber incident reports and ransom payment reports that is known to be personal information of a specific individual or information that identifies a specific individual that is not directly related to a cybersecurity threat.section 1504(b) of this title
Other procedural measures directly necessary to implement subsection (a).

children.heading: Third party report submission and ransom payment
children.label: d Third party report submission and ransom payment

children.heading: Report submission
children.label: 1 Report submission
children.text: A covered entity that is required to submit a covered cyber incident report or a ransom payment report may use a third party, such as an incident response company, insurance provider, service provider, Information Sharing and Analysis Organization, or law firm, to submit the required report under subsection (a).

children.heading: Ransom payment
children.label: 2 Ransom payment
children.text: If a covered entity impacted by a ransomware attack uses a third party to make a ransom payment, the third party shall not be required to submit a ransom payment report for itself under subsection (a)(2).

children.heading: Duty to report
children.label: 3 Duty to report
children.text: Third-party reporting under this subparagraph does not relieve a covered entity from the duty to comply with the requirements for covered cyber incident report or ransom payment report submission.4So in original. Probably should be “subsection”.4

children.heading: Responsibility to advise
children.label: 4 Responsibility to advise
children.text: Any third party used by a covered entity that knowingly makes a ransom payment on behalf of a covered entity impacted by a ransomware attack shall advise the impacted covered entity of the responsibilities of the impacted covered entity regarding reporting ransom payments under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Agency shall conduct an outreach and education campaign to inform likely covered entities, entities that offer or advertise as a service to customers to make or facilitate ransom payments on behalf of covered entities impacted by ransomware attacks and other appropriate entities of the requirements of paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) of subsection (a).

children.label: A
children.text: An overview of the final rule issued pursuant to subsection (b).

children.label: B
children.text: An overview of mechanisms to submit to the Agency covered cyber incident reports, ransom payment reports, and information relating to the disclosure, retention, and use of covered cyber incident reports and ransom payment reports under this section.

children.label: C
children.text: An overview of the protections afforded to covered entities for complying with the requirements under paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) of subsection (a).

children.label: D
children.text: An overview of the steps taken under when a covered entity is not in compliance with the reporting requirements under subsection (a).section 681d of this title

children.label: E
children.text: Specific outreach to cybersecurity vendors, cyber incident response providers, cybersecurity insurance entities, and other entities that may support covered entities.

children.label: F
children.text: An overview of the privacy and civil liberties requirements in this part.

children.label: A
children.text: the Critical Infrastructure Partnership Advisory Council established under ;section 451 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: Information Sharing and Analysis Organizations;

children.label: C
children.text: trade associations;

children.label: D
children.text: information sharing and analysis centers;

children.label: E
children.text: sector coordinating councils; and

children.label: F
children.text: any other entity as determined appropriate by the Director.

children.heading: Exemption
children.label: f Exemption
children.text: Sections 3506(c), 3507, 3508, and 3509 of title 44 shall not apply to any action to carry out this section.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: g Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall affect the authorities of the Federal Government to implement the requirements of Executive Order 14028 (86 Fed. Reg. 26633; relating to improving the nation’s cybersecurity), including changes to the Federal Acquisition Regulations and remedies to include suspension and debarment.

children.heading: Savings provision
children.label: h Savings provision
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to supersede or to abrogate, modify, or otherwise limit the authority that is vested in any officer or any agency of the United States Government to regulate or take action with respect to the cybersecurity of an entity.
